data.title: Zakat Cal with Dynamic Wealth
data.statement:
In Islam, Zakat becomes obligatory when a person owns wealth equal to or above the Nisab (85 grams of gold) for one full lunar year (12 months).

During the year, the person may receive money, trade goods, or gold.
All additions before the Zakat due date must be included in the final calculation.

Debts must be subtracted before calculating Zakat.

Zakat rate = 2.5%
data.stubGenerator:
read G:int
read M:int
loopline M w:int
read D:int
write 0
data.inputDescription:
G
M
w1 w2 w3 ... wM
D

G = price of 1 gram of gold
M = number of months (12 ≤ M ≤ 120)
wi = wealth gained in month i (can be negative for loss)
D = total debt at the end of the year
data.outputDescription:
Compute Nisab = 85 × G
Compute Total Wealth = sum(w1...wM) − D
If Total Wealth < Nisab → print: "No Zakat required"
Otherwise print: "Zakat: X"

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

In Islam, Zakat becomes obligatory when a person owns wealth equal to or above the Nisab (85 grams of gold) for one full lunar year (12 months).

During the year, the person may receive money, trade goods, or gold.
All additions before the Zakat due date must be included in the final calculation.

Debts must be subtracted before calculating Zakat.

Zakat rate = 2.5%
Input
G
M
w1 w2 w3 ... wM
D

G = price of 1 gram of gold
M = number of months (12 ≤ M ≤ 120)
wi = wealth gained in month i (can be negative for loss)
D = total debt at the end of the year
Output
Compute Nisab = 85 × G
Compute Total Wealth = sum(w1...wM) − D
If Total Wealth < Nisab → print: "No Zakat required"
Otherwise print: "Zakat: X"
Constraints
1 ≤ G ≤ 10^6
12 ≤ M ≤ 120
-10^9 ≤ wi ≤ 10^9
0 ≤ D ≤ 10^12
Example
Input
70
12
1000 1000 1000 1000 1000 1000 1000 1000 1000 1000 1000 1000
0
Output
Zakat: 300.00
